result.sentence: In Genshin Impact, by checking whether the "A Tea Party Beneath the Moon" event is currently active, you can determine if you are able to obtain the dish "Fine Tea, Full Moon".

In the guide, there are three methods for connecting a Nintendo Switch to a PC: transferring screenshots or videos, using the Switch as a controller for PC games, and streaming or capturing gameplay with a capture card.
The guide assumes the user wants the common case of displaying or recording Nintendo Switch gameplay on a PC.

By gathering the required equipment—Nintendo Switch with dock, the dock’s HDMI cable, the dock’s AC power adapter, a USB capture card, a USB cable for the capture card, and a PC or laptop—you can prepare everything needed to connect the Switch to the PC.
By placing the Nintendo Switch into its dock, the console becomes securely positioned and ready to output video through the dock.
By plugging the Switch dock’s AC power adapter into the dock and into a wall outlet, the dock is supplied with electricity.
By plugging one end of the HDMI cable into the dock’s HDMI OUT port, the dock can send its video signal through the cable.
By plugging the other end of the HDMI cable into the capture card’s HDMI IN port, the capture card receives the Nintendo Switch’s video signal.
By connecting another HDMI cable from the capture card’s HDMI OUT port to a TV (when available), you enable TV passthrough so the TV can display the Switch’s video without delay.
By connecting the capture card to the PC using the supplied USB cable, the PC can detect the capture card as a video input device.
The PC automatically recognizes the connected capture card as a video device.
By downloading the capture card’s software (such as Elgato Game Capture HD or OBS Studio), you obtain the program needed to manage the video input.
By installing the capture card’s software on the PC, the program becomes available for use.
By launching the capture software, you open the application that can display and record the video source.
By selecting the capture card as the video source within the software, the Switch’s video feed is displayed in the application.
After configuring the software, the Nintendo Switch’s screen appears on the PC within the capture application.
With the video feed visible on the PC, you can record, stream, or use the PC monitor to play Nintendo Switch games.
If the goal is to transfer files or use the Nintendo Switch as a controller for PC games, a different set of steps must be followed.
